content.title: Volatility Kills
content.summary: {'A': '尽管乍得首都发生枪战、肯尼亚发生骚乱、津巴布韦通货膨胀严重，但整体而言，撒哈拉以南非洲的经济比几年前好转。世界银行最近报告称，该地区在2006年实现了5.6%的可观增长率，并且在1995年至2005年的增长率比之前几十年更高。然而，发展经济学家们仍然面临一个重要问题，即这种复苏是否会持续，以便非洲人能够通过增长摆脱贫困，而该地区近7.44亿人口中约40%的人每天生活费不到1美元。然而，仔细观察后发现，乐观情绪可能是短暂的，因为经济波动的毁灭性模式已经持续了几十年。', 'B': '非洲国家在经济好时期的增长速度与亚洲国家和其他发展中国家一样快，但之后他们的增长却崩溃了，如何防止这种崩溃可能与促进增长同样重要。如果没有发生这些崩溃，撒哈拉以南非洲48个国家每个公民的国内生产总值水平将会高出三分之一。', 'C': '非洲的经济增长可能不可持续，因为缺乏必要的基础设施和经济基本面，而且资源丰富的国家可能会因为经济繁荣而无法实施良好的经济实践。', 'D': '这段话主要讲了保罗·科利尔（Paul Collier）对于非洲国家经济波动性问题的分析，他提出了一系列政策建议，包括改革贸易措施、更合理分配援助以及在某些情况下持续军事干预，以避免他认为世界上最贫困的地区，主要是非洲，与其他发展中国家（如印度和中国）迅速拉开差距。', 'E': '作者发现糟糕的治理是国家无法充分利用经济繁荣带来的收入繁荣的主要原因，而且民主政府往往使经济繁荣之后的局势更加糟糕。', 'F': '作者主张非洲国家应该建立一系列的标准和规范来加强政府，其中之一是用拍卖取代贿赂来分配矿权，另一个是充分征税出口收入。他引用了刚果民主共和国的例子，该国在2006年从矿产出口中获得2亿美元，但只收取了86000美元的税收。他认为，“如果一个国家做对了这些事情，它将会发展；如果做错了，就不会发展。”', 'G': '为了鼓励改革，Collier建议G8国家同意将这些措施作为跨国公司在非洲开展业务的自愿指南，例如，公司只能通过由国际验证组监督的拍卖方式进入新合同。', 'H': '这段话主要讲述了作者认为，与提高援助水平相比，采取的措施更为重要，而经济学家杰弗里·D·萨克斯和名人活动家波诺等人则强调提高援助水平。作者坚持认为，首先安哥拉获得数十亿美元的石油收入，无论援助金额增加或减少几亿美元都是次要的。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: . Despite gun battles in the capital of Chad, rioting in Kenya, and Galloping inflation in Zimbabwe, the economies of sub-Saharan Africa are, as a whole, in better shape than they were a few years ago. The World Bank has reported recently that this part of the continent experienced a respectable growth rate of 5.6 percent in 2006 and a higher rate from 1995 to 2005 than in previous decades. The bank has given a cautions assessment that the region may have reached a turning point. An overriding question for developmental economists remains whether the upswing will continue so Africans can grow their way out of poverty that relegates some 40 percent of the nearly 744 million in that region to living on less than a dollar a day. The optimism, when inspected more closely, may be short-lived because of the persistence of a devastating pattern of economic volatility that has lingered for decades.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: "In reality, African countries grow as fast as Asian countries and other developing countries during the good times, but afterward they see growth collapses," comments Jorge Arbache, a senior World Bank economist. "How to prevent collapses may be as important as promoting growth." If these collapses had not occurred, he observes, the level of gross domestic product for each citizen of the 48 nations of sub -Saharan Africa would have been third higher.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: . the prerequisites to prevent the next crash are not in place, according to a World Bank study issued in January, Is Africa's Recent Growth Robust? The growth period that began in 1995, driven by a commodities boom spurred in particular by demand from China, may not be sustainable, because the economic fundamentals- new investment and the ability to stave off inflation, among other factors-are absent. The region lacks the necessary infrastructure that would encourage investors to look to Africa to find the next Bengaluru ( Bangalore ) or Shenzhen, a November report from the bank concludes. For sub–Saharan countries rich in oil and other resources, a boom period may even undermine efforts to institute sound economic practices.. From 1996 to 2005, with growth accelerating, measures of governance– factors such as political stability, rule of law, and control of corruption- actually worsened, especially for countries endowed with abundant mineral resources, the January report notes.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: . Perhaps the most incisive analysis of the volatility question comes from Paul Collier, a longtime specialist in African economics at the University of Oxford and author of the recent book The Bottom Billion. He advocates a range of options that the U.S. and other nations could adopt when formulating policy toward African countries. They include revamped trade measures, better-apportioned aid, and sustained military intervention in certain instances, to avert what he sees as a rapidly accelerating divergence of the world's poorest, primarily in Africa, from the rest of the world, even other developing nations such India and China.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: Collier finds that bad governance is the main reason countries fail to take advantage of the revenue bonanza that results from a boom. moreover, a democratic government, he adds, often makes the aftermath of a boom worse. "Instead of democracy disciplining governments to manage these resource booms well, what happens is that the resource revenues corrupt the normal functioning of democracy-unless you stop ( them from) corrupting the normal function of democracy with sufficient checks and balances", he said at a talk ion January at the Carnegie Council in New York City.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: Collier advocates that African nations institute an array of standards and codes to bolster governments, one of which would substitute auctions for bribes in apportioning mineral rights and another of which would tax export revenues adequately. He cites the Democratic Republic of the Congo, which took in $ 200 million from mineral exports in 2006 yet collected only $86000 in royalties for its treasury. "If a nation gets these points right, " he argues, "It's going to develop. If it gets them wrong, it won't."

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: . To encourage reform, Collier recommends that the G8 nations agree to accept these measures as voluntary guidelines for multinationals doing business in Africa- companies, for instance, would only enter new contracts through auctions monitored by an international verification group. Such an agreement would follow the examples of the so-called Kimberley Process, which has effectively undercut the trade in blood diamonds, and the Extractive Industries Transparency Initiative, in which a government must report to its citizens the revenues it receives from sales of natural resources.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: . These measures, he says, are more important than elevating aid levels, an approach emphasized by economist Jeffrey D. Sachs of Columbia University and celebrity activists such as Bono. Collier insists that first Angola receives tens of billions of dollars in oil revenue and whether it gets a few hundred million more or less in aid is really second-order.

questions.analysis: 题干中的关键词是"unexpectedly opposite result"。我们可以通过同义替换的方式在原文中找到相关信息。在原文中，我们可以找到同义表达"rapidly accelerating divergence"。这个表达意味着世界上最贫穷的地区，主要是非洲，与其他地区，甚至是其他发展中国家（如印度和中国）之间的差距正在迅速扩大。在这段原文中，提到了一位专家Paul Collier，他是牛津大学非洲经济学专家，也是最近出版的书《The Bottom Billion》的作者。他提出了一系列政策建议，包括改革贸易措施、更合理分配的援助以及在某些情况下持续的军事干预，以避免这种差距的扩大。因此，答案是Paul Collier。

questions.title: Estimated more productive outcomes if it were not for sudden economic downturns
questions.analysis:
题干中提到了"Estimated more productive outcomes if it were not for sudden economic downturns"，我们可以将这个信息与原文进行匹配。

在原文中，有一句话提到了经济增长的崩溃："“In reality, African countries grow as fast as Asian countries and other developing countries during the good times, but afterward they see growth collapses,” comments Jorge Arbache, a senior World Bank economist." 这句话中提到了经济增长的崩溃，与题干中的"sudden economic downturns"相对应。

而在同一段落中，还有一句话提到了如果这些崩溃没有发生，每个非洲撒哈拉以南国家的公民的国内生产总值水平将会高出三分之一："If these collapses had not occurred, he observes, the level of gross domestic product for each citizen of the 48 nations of sub -Saharan Africa would have been third higher." 这句话中提到了每个公民的国内生产总值水平，与题干中的"more productive outcomes"相对应。

根据原文，我们可以得出结论，这段话是由Jorge Arbache，一位世界银行的高级经济学家发表的评论。因此，答案是"Jorge Arbache"。

所以，我们可以通过对比题干和原文中的信息，找到答案"Jorge Arbache"。

content.title: The sense of flavour 2
content.summary: {'A': '人类获得味觉的原因是为了避免中毒，味觉可以帮助我们区分有益于我们的食物和有害的食物，但与人类嗅觉系统相比，味觉的检测能力有限，食物的香气可以占到其味道的90%。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了喝、吸或咀嚼物质会释放出挥发性气体，这些气体通过口腔和鼻孔流出，进入位于鼻子底部、眼睛之间的嗅觉上皮细胞层，大脑将来自嗅觉上皮的复杂气味信号与来自舌头的简单味觉信号结合起来，为口中的物质赋予味道，并决定是否想要吃下它。', 'C': '这段话主要讲述了婴儿对甜味的喜好和对苦味的排斥，以及人的食物偏好和个性是在幼年时期通过社会化过程形成的，幼儿可以根据周围人的饮食习惯学会喜欢辣味食物、清淡健康食品或快餐，而人类的嗅觉尚未完全被理解，它受心理因素和期望的影响很大，人们可以逐渐习惯于恶臭或好闻的气味，不再注意曾经被认为是压倒性的气味。', 'D': '气味和记忆有着紧密的联系，童年的美食味道会留下深刻的印象，成年人常常会回忆起这些“安慰食物”，这也是快餐连锁店努力推广的原因。', 'E': '人类对于味道的渴望在历史上一直是一个被忽视和未经审视的重要力量，香料贸易改变了皇家帝国、探索未知土地、伟大的宗教和哲学，而今天，味道在世界市场上的影响同样决定性，企业帝国的兴衰往往取决于他们产品的口味。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了香精行业的起源和发展，以及人们如何从香水公司获取了最早的风味添加剂，并介绍了德国化学工业在20世纪初成为风味生产的领导者，并提到了一位德国科学家偶然发现了甲基葡萄酸酯，成为了人工葡萄汁的主要调味剂。', 'G': '人们在食物中最看重的品质，即其味道，通常以一种无法用传统的烹饪术语如盎司或茶匙来衡量的微不足道的数量存在。如今的先进光谱仪、气相色谱仪和顶空蒸汽分析仪可以提供食物味道成分的详细图谱，检测到每十亿分之一的化学香气。然而，人类的嗅觉仍然比任何已发明的机器更敏感，能够察觉到每万亿分之一的香气。复杂的香气，如咖啡或烤肉的香气，可能由近千种不同化学物质的气体组成。提供青椒主要味道的化学物质可以在每十亿分之0.02的数量中被尝到；一滴就足以给填满五个中等大小游泳池所需的水增添味道。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Scientists now believe that human beings acquired the sense of taste as a way to avoid being poisoned. Edible plants generally taste sweet; deadly ones, bitter. Taste is supposed to help us differentiate food that's good for us from food that's not. The taste buds on our tongues can detect the presence of half a dozen or so basic tastes, including sweet, sour, bitter, salty, and umami (a taste discovered by Japanese researchers, a rich and full sense of deliciousness triggered by amino acids in foods such as shellfish, mushrooms, potatoes, and seaweed). Taste buds offers a limited means of detection, however, compared with the human olfactory system, which can perceive thousands of different chemical aromas. Indeed, 'flavor' is primarily the smell of gases being released by the chemicals you've just put in your mouth. The aroma of food can be responsible for as much as 90% of its flavor.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: The act of drinking, sucking or chewing a substance releases its volatile gases. They flow out of the mouth and up the nostrils, or up the passageway at the back of the mouth, to a thin layer of nerve cells called the olfactory epithelium, located at the base of the nose, right between the eyes. The brain combines the complex smell signals from the epithelium with the simple taste signals from the tongue, assigns a flavor to what's in your mouth, and decides if it's something you want to eat.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: Babies like sweet tastes and reject bitter ones; we know this because scientists have rubbed various flavors inside the mouths of infants and then recorded their facial reactions. A person's food preferences, like his or her personality, are formed during the first few years of life, through a process of socialization. Toddlers can learn to enjoy hot and spicy food, bland health food, or fast food, depending upon what the people around them eat. The human sense of smell is still not fully understood. It is greatly affected by psychological factors and expectations. The mind filters out the overwhelming majority of chemical aromas that surround us, focusing intently on some, ignoring others. People can grow accustomed to bad smells or good smells; they stop noticing what once seemed overpowering.

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: Aroma and memory are somehow inextricably linked. A smell can suddenly evoke a long-forgotten moment. The flavours of childhood foods seem to leave an indelible mark, and adults often return to them, without always knowing why. These 'comfort foods' become a source of pleasure and reassurance a fact that fast-food chains work hard to promote Childhood memories of Happy Meals can translate into frequent adult visits to McDonald's', like those of the chain's 'heavy users', the customers who eat there four or five times a week.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text:
The human craving for flavour has been a large unacknowledged and unexamined force in history. Royal empires have been built, unexplored lands have been traversed, great religions and philosophies have been forever changed by the spice trade. In 1492, Christopher
Columbus set sail in order to try to find new seasonings and thus to make his fortune with this most desired commodity of that time. Today, the influence of flavour in the world marketplace is no less decisive. The rise and fall of corporate empires – soft-drink companies, snack-food companies, and fast-food chains – is frequently determined by how their products taste.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: The flavor industry emerged in the mid-1800s, as processed foods began to be manufactured on a large scale. Recognizing the need for flavor additives, the early food processors turned to perfume companies that had years of experience working with essential oils and volatile aromas. The great perfume houses of England, France, and the Netherlands produced many of the first flavor compounds. In the early part of the 20th century, Germany's powerful chemical industry assumed the lead in flavour production. Legend has it that a German scientist discovered methyl anthranilate, one of the first artificial flavours, by accident while mixing chemicals in his laboratory. Suddenly, the lab was filled with the sweet smell of grapes. Methyl anthranilate later became the chief flavoring compound of manufactured grape juice.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: The quality that people seek most of all in a food, its flavour, is usually present in a quantity too infinitesimal to be measured by any traditional culinary terms such as ounces or teaspoons.Today's sophisticated spectrometers, gas chromatograph, and headspace vapor analyzers provide a detailed map of a food's flavour components, detecting chemical aromas in amounts as low as one part per billion. The human nose, however, is still more sensitive than any machine yet invented. A nose can detect aromas present in quantities of a few parts per trillion. Complex aromas, such as those of coffee or roasted meat, may be composed of gases from nearly a thousand different chemicals. The chemical that provides the dominant flavour of bell pepper can be tasted in amounts as low as 0.02 parts per billion; one drop is sufficient to add flavour to the amount of water needed to fill five average-sized swimming pools

content.title: Digital diet
content.summary: {'A': '数字化时代的发展使得电子邮件、数据处理和网络搜索等数字活动消耗了大量的能源，这对环境造成了巨大的影响，因此互联网和计算机公司有动力降低能源消耗。', 'B': '这段话主要讲述了数据中心中最大的能源消耗不是来自计算机本身，而是来自于为了防止它们过热而需要的空调能源消耗。', 'C': 'Google为了保证网页能够在几秒钟内加载完成，必须维护数十万台计算机服务器，因此他们正在努力提高数据中心的能效，以减少能源消耗。', 'D': '这段话主要讲述了电脑行业为了节省能源而采取的一些技巧，包括减少电力转换次数、重新布置服务器和冷却机制以及使用虚拟化软件等。这些技巧使得惠普公司将分布在全球的86个数据中心整合为仅有三个，并备有三个备份。', 'E': '该段话主要讲述了计算机芯片领域正在解决能源问题，通过采用多核技术和纳米级电路缩小等方式来节省能源消耗。', 'F': '这段话主要讲述了使用这种芯片后，更多的个人电脑将符合各种效率标准，如能源之星认证和电子产品环境评估工具标准，并且一些组织还致力于在2010年前将所有计算机的功耗减少50%。', 'G': '大部分操作系统内置的睡眠模式和其他节能工具可以节省能源，但大约90%的计算机没有启用这些设置，如果正确启用，它们可以防止每年从发电厂排放数千公斤的二氧化碳，但如果关闭或拔掉电脑（唯一不消耗电力的方式）不是一个选择，那么最环保的方式可能是利用这些浪费的计算周期来帮助模拟气候变化的后果。', 'H': '这段话主要介绍了一个名为CO2 Stats的免费工具，可以嵌入到任何网站中，用于计算使用该网站所产生的二氧化碳排放量。'}

paragraphs.label: A
paragraphs.text: Telecommuting, Internet shopping and online meetings may save energy as compared with in-person alternatives, but as the digital age moves on, its green reputation is turning a lot browner. E-mailing, number crunching and Web searches in the U.S. consumed as much as 61 billion kilowatt-hours last year, or 1.5 per cent of the nation's electricity-half of which comes from coal. In 2005 the computers of the world ate up 123 billion kilowatt-hours of energy, a number that will double by 2010 if present trends continue, according to Jonathan Koomey, a staff scientist at Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. As a result, the power bill to run a computer over its lifetime will surpass the cost of buying the machine in the first place giving Internet and computer companies a business reason to cut energy costs, as well as an environmental one.

paragraphs.label: B
paragraphs.text: One of the biggest energy sinks comes not from the computers themselves but from the air-conditioning needed to keep them from overheating. For every kilowatt-hour of energy used for computing in a data centre, another kilowatt-hour is required to cool the furnace-like racks of servers.

paragraphs.label: C
paragraphs.text: For Internet giant Google, this reality has driven efforts such as the installation of a solar array that can provide 30 per cent of the peak power needs of its Mountain View, Calif., headquarters as well as increased purchases of renewable energy. But to deliver Web pages within seconds, the firm must maintain hundreds of thousands of computer servers in cavernous buildings. "It's a good thing to worry about server energy efficiency," remarks Google's green energy czar Bill Weihl. "We are actively working to maximize the efficiency of our data centres, which account for most of the energy Google consumes worldwide." Google will funnel some of its profits into a new effort, dubbed RE

paragraphs.label: D
paragraphs.text: In the meantime, the industry as a whole has employed a few tricks to save watts. Efforts include cutting down on the number of transformations the electricity itself must undergo before achieving the correct operating voltage; rearranging the stacks of servers and the mechanics of their cooling; and using software to create multiple "virtual" computers, rather than having to deploy several real ones. Such virtualization has allowed computer maker Hewlett-Packard to consolidate 86 data centers spread throughout the world to just three, with three backups, says Pat Tiernan, the firm's vice president of social and environmental responsibility.

paragraphs.label: E
paragraphs.text: The industry is also tackling the energy issue at the computer-chip level. With every doubling of processing power in recent years has come a doubling in power consumption. But to save energy, chipmakers such as Intel and AMD have shifted to so-called multicore technology, which packs multiple processors into one circuit rather than separating them."When we moved to multicore-away from a linear focus on megahertz and gigahertz— and throttled down microprocessors, the energy savings were pretty substantial," says Allyson Klein, Intel's marketing manager for its Ecotech Initiative. Chipmakers continue to shrink circuits on the nanoscale as well, which means a chip needs less electricity" to deliver the same performance, she adds.

paragraphs.label: F
paragraphs.text: With such chips, more personal computers will meet various efficiency standards, such as Energy Star compliance (which mandates that a desktop consume no more than 65 watts). The federal government, led by agencies such as NASA and the Department of Defense may soon require all their purchases to meet the Electronic Product Environmental Assessment Tool standard. And Google, Intel and others have formed the Climate Savers Computing Initiative, an effort to cut power consumption from all computers by 50 per cent by 2010.

paragraphs.label: G
paragraphs.text: Sleep modes and other power management tools built into most operating systems can offer savings today. Yet about 90 per cent of computers do not have such settings enabled, according to Klein. Properly activated, they would prevent a computer from leading to the emission of thousands of kilograms of carbon dioxide from power plants every year. But if powering down or unplugging the computer (the only way it uses zero power) is not an option, then perhaps the most environmentally friendly use of all those wasted computing cycles is in helping to model climate change. The University of Oxford's ClimatePrediction.net offers an opportunity to at least predict the consequences of all that coal burning.

paragraphs.label: H
paragraphs.text: CO2 Stats is a free tool that can be embedded into any Website to calculate the carbon dioxide emissions associated with using it. That estimate is based on an assumption of 300 watts of power consumed by the personal computer, network and server involved- or 16.5 milligrams of CO2 emitted every second of use. "The typical carbon footprint is roughly equivalent to 1.5 people breathing," says physicist Alexander Wissner-Gross of Harvard University, who co-created the Web tool.

题干中提到了"Figuring ways to optimize the utilization of energy in certain significant departments in the company"，我们需要找到与之相关的信息。在原文中，有一句话提到了Google正在努力提高数据中心的效率，这些数据中心消耗了Google全球大部分的能源。因此，我们可以确定这里的"significant departments"指的就是Google的数据中心。而在同一句话中，提到了Google正在积极努力提高数据中心的效率，这与题干中的"optimize the utilization of energy"意思相符。因此，我们可以确定这里的"optimize"指的就是提高数据中心的效率。

接下来，我们需要找到与"optimize the utilization of energy"相关的具体人物。在原文中，有一句话提到了Google的绿色能源负责人Bill Weihl，他提到了关于服务器能源效率的问题。因此，我们可以确定Bill Weihl与"optimize the utilization of energy"相关。

综上所述，答案为Bill Weihl。

central_sentences.translation: 为了节省能源，英特尔和AMD等芯片制造商已经转向所谓的多核技术，将多个处理器集成到一个电路中，而不是将它们分开。“当我们转向多核技术，不再线性关注兆赫和千兆赫时，调低微处理器的速度，节能效果相当显著，”英特尔的生态技术倡议市场经理Allyson Klein表示。她补充说，芯片制造商继续在纳米尺度上缩小电路，这意味着芯片需要更少的电力来提供相同的性能。

target_sen.target_sen: The industry is also tackling the energy issue at the computer-chip level.

But to save energy, chipmakers such as Intel and AMD have shifted to so-called multicore technology, which packs multiple processors into one circuit rather than separating them.“When we moved to multicore-away from a linear focus on megahertz and gigahertz— and throttled down microprocessors, the energy savings were pretty substantial,” says Allyson Klein, Intel’s marketing manager for its Ecotech Initiative.

But to save energy, chipmakers such as Intel and AMD have shifted to so-called multicore technology, which packs multiple processors into one circuit rather than separating them.“When we moved to multicore-away from a linear focus on megahertz and gigahertz— and throttled down microprocessors, the energy savings were pretty substantial,” says Allyson Klein, Intel’s marketing manager for its Ecotech Initiative.

But to save energy, chipmakers such as Intel and AMD have shifted to so-called multicore technology, which packs multiple processors into one circuit rather than separating them.“When we moved to multicore-away from a linear focus on megahertz and gigahertz— and throttled down microprocessors, the energy savings were pretty substantial,” says Allyson Klein, Intel’s marketing manager for its Ecotech Initiative.

But to save energy, chipmakers such as Intel and AMD have shifted to so-called multicore technology, which packs multiple processors into one circuit rather than separating them.“When we moved to multicore-away from a linear focus on megahertz and gigahertz— and throttled down microprocessors, the energy savings were pretty substantial,” says Allyson Klein, Intel’s marketing manager for its Ecotech Initiative.
Chipmakers continue to shrink circuits on the nanoscale as well, which means a chip needs less electricity” to deliver the same performance, she adds.
